case.divain.why.text: the most direct structural threat. Same price point (€15-30). Digital-first model.
case.forces.1.text: Spain's dupe segment fell from 37% to 13% of consumers between 2018 and 2023 — a 65% drop in Equivalenza's core market.
case.forces.1.title: 1. Dupe segment collapse
case.forces.2.text: Premium brands discounting from above (more accessible). Zara and Mercadona upgrading from below (more aspirational). Equivalenza squeezed in the middle.
case.forces.2.title: 2. Sandwich effect
case.forces.3.text: Divain, Dossier, Adopt — operate without physical costs and invest more in marketing per euro of revenue.
case.forces.3.title: 3. Digital-native competitors
case.persona.intro: Creative professional in Madrid. Moderate-to-good income. University-educated, urban, socially active.
case.subtitle: Everything you need to know about the company, the market and the competition.

dl.card.pdf.desc: The complete mid-term — 13 pages, ~2000 words, Harvard references, cover, TOC, color-coded TOWS matrix, appendix with key-data table and buyer persona visual.

dl.howto.p1: Do NOT submit exactly as is. The goal is to read, understand the analysis, and rewrite in your own voice. Change the examples, reorder paragraphs, add your perspective.
dl.howto.p2: This ensures: (1) the text sounds like you, (2) it passes Turnitin, (3) you can defend each point orally.
dl.warning.p: Q3 (Marketing Problem), Q4 (TOWS) and Q5 (Recommendation) cannot use AI directly. These sections must be entirely yours. Use this material to understand the methodology and then write on your own.

routes.aulas.curso.comercio-internaciona: Business Administration · Trade theories, entry strategies, international finance, logistics and marketing
routes.aulas.curso.comportamento-do-cons: Business Administration · Influencing factors, 5-stage buying process, STP segmentation, personas and omnichannel behaviour
routes.aulas.curso.estrategia-corporativ: Advanced Business Administration · External and internal analysis, corporate strategies and implementation
routes.aulas.curso.marketing-internacion: Business Administration · Global STP, international 4Ps mix, market entry modes, Hofstede and corporate diplomacy
routes.aulas.curso.pesquisa-de-marketing: Business Administration · Research process, qualitative vs quantitative, sampling, data collection methods and analysis
routes.aulas.curso.sistemas-de-informaca: Business Administration · IS types, ERP/CRM, BI, information security and cloud
routes.biblioteca.new.title: New Bookmark
routes.biblioteca.title: Library · Bookmarks
routes.case.title: Case · Equivalenza Deep Dive
routes.course.title: Course · Theory
routes.dl.title: Downloads · Assignment Center
routes.escola.caderno.title: My Notebook
routes.escola.curso.analise-financeira.d: Ratios, cash flows and valuation — how to read financial statements.
routes.escola.curso.analise-investimento: Economic evaluation of investment projects: cash flows, NPV, IRR, payback, risk and uncertainty analysis (sensitivity, Monte Carlo, real options), cost of capital WACC, and optimal capital structure.
Business Administration · Capital budgeting, NPV/IRR, risk, cost of capital
routes.escola.curso.comercio-internacion: Foundations, strategies and operations of international trade: classical theories (Smith's absolute advantage, Ricardo's comparative advantage, Heckscher-Ohlin, Stolper-Samuelson), economic integration and WTO/GATT, Gereffi's global value chains, market entry modes and Uppsala incremental internationalisation, FX and Incoterms 2020, UCP600 letters of credit and trade finance, international marketing (4Ps, Hofstede, Trompenaars), CISG and ICC/UNCITRAL arbitration.
Business Administration · Trade theories, entry strategies, international finance, logistics and marketing
routes.escola.curso.comportamento-do-con: The analytical course on why consumers choose, buy, use and dispose of products: Kotler's four blocks of influencing factors (cultural — culture, subculture, social class; social — reference groups, family, roles and status; personal — age, occupation, lifestyle, personality, self-concept; psychological — Maslow motivation, perception, learning, beliefs and attitudes), the five-stage buying decision process (need recognition, information search, evaluation of alternatives, purchase decision, post-purchase behaviour with cognitive dissonance), types of problem solving (extensive, limited and routinised response behaviour depending on involvement level), STP segmentation with demographic, psychographic, behavioural, geographic and benefit criteria, RFM scoring for CRM, persona construction, and digital omnichannel behaviour (customer journey, mobile-first, social commerce, live shopping, showrooming, webrooming, personalisation under GDPR).
Business Administration · Influencing factors, 5-stage buying process, STP segmentation, personas and omnichannel behaviour
routes.escola.curso.comportamento-organi: How people think, feel and act in organizations: personality, motivation and individual perception (Big Five, Maslow, Herzberg, McGregor, Self-Determination Theory), group and team dynamics (Tuckman, Belbin, groupthink, social loafing), structure, culture and power (Mintzberg, Schein, Hofstede, French-Raven), and leadership, empowerment and conflict management (Fiedler, Path-Goal, Bass, Thomas-Kilmann).
Business Administration · People, teams, culture and leadership
routes.escola.curso.contabilidade-gerenc: How managers use cost information to decide, plan and control: cost classification (fixed, variable, direct, indirect), cost-volume-profit (CVP) analysis and break-even, operating budgets and budgetary control, ABC and Balanced Scorecard.
Types of companies, commercial contracts, intellectual property and arbitration in Portugal.
routes.escola.curso.empreendedorismo.des: The most practical BA course: how to turn an idea into a sustainable business. Covers entrepreneurial mindset (effectuation, journey, biases), Business Model Canvas, problem validation and MVP (4 types), full business plan and 3-5 minute pitch to investors.
routes.escola.curso.empreendedorismo.tag: Business Administration · Mindset, BMC, MVP, investor pitch
routes.escola.curso.estrategia-corporati: The advanced strategy course for C-level decision-makers: in-depth external analysis (PESTEL, Porter Five Forces, value chain, strategic groups), resource-based internal analysis (VRIO, RBV, strategic SWOT, McKinsey 7S), corporate strategies (Ansoff, BCG and GE-McKinsey portfolio, vertical/horizontal integration, diversification), and implementation (Balanced Scorecard, OKRs, McKinsey 7S, strategic control and change management).

Advanced Business Administration · External analysis (PESTEL, 5 Forces, value chain), internal analysis (VRIO, McKinsey 7S), corporate strategies (Ansoff, BCG, integration) and implementation (BSC, OKRs)
routes.escola.curso.gestao-financeira-em: Corporate finance in practice: how to read and build the three financial statements (Income Statement, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow Statement), analyze liquidity, profitability and leverage ratios, evaluate investment projects with NPV/IRR/payback, and decide the optimal capital structure (WACC, equity vs. debt).
routes.escola.curso.gestao-inovacao.desc: Innovation is not just ideas: it is a system. Master end-to-end innovation management — Drucker, Christensen, design thinking, lean startup, Stage-Gate funnel, portfolio and ecosystems.
routes.escola.curso.gestao-inovacao.tagl: Business Administration · Innovation, design thinking and ecosystems
routes.escola.curso.gestao-mudanca.descr: Kotter 8-step model, Prosci ADKAR, resistance management and Schein organizational culture.
routes.escola.curso.gestao-mudanca.title: Organizational Change Management
routes.escola.curso.gestao-operacoes.des: How to design, run and improve the processes that turn inputs into outputs: Porter's Value Chain and operations strategy (cost/quality/speed/flexibility trade-offs), process design and flow analysis (bottleneck, Little's Law, layouts), Toyota Production System and Six Sigma (DMAIC) for operational excellence, and planning & control (MRP, EOQ, ABC, SPC, queuing theory).
routes.escola.curso.gestao-operacoes.tag: Business Administration · Processes, lean, six sigma, planning and control
routes.escola.curso.gestao-qualidade.des: How to build a quality culture across the entire organisation: Deming, Juran, Ishikawa and Crosby principles, tools (Pareto, fishbone, SPC, FMEA), ISO 9001/14001/45001 management systems with PDCA and audits, and service quality via ServQual, lean services, six sigma and the EFQM model.
routes.escola.curso.gestao-qualidade.tag: Business Administration · TQM, tools, ISO 9001 and service excellence
routes.escola.curso.inovacao-empreendedo: Entrepreneurial mindset (resilience, opportunity spotting), design thinking as user-centred innovation method, idea validation with MVP, customer development and lean startup, and funding + pitch to scale.
Sources of capital (bootstrapping, business angels, VC), pitch deck vs elevator pitch, investor metrics (TAM/SAM/SOM, LTV/CAC, runway) and how to approach business angels.
University · Mindset, design thinking, validation and funding
routes.escola.curso.introducao-ao-direit: Sources of law (Constitution, statute, custom), persons and legal capacity, obligations and contracts, and civil liability. Foundation for contracts, risk and compliance.
routes.escola.curso.lideranca-coaching.d: How to lead people effectively in the modern era: foundations of leadership theories (traits, behaviours, situational — Fiedler, Hersey-Blanchard), executive coaching practice (Whitmore's GROW model, SBI feedback), leader emotional intelligence (Goleman's 5 pillars and the neuroscience of self-regulation), and succession development (leadership pipelines, mentoring, sponsorship and high-potential programs).
routes.escola.curso.lideranca-coaching.t: Business Administration · Situational leadership, executive coaching, emotional intelligence and succession
routes.escola.curso.logistica.descriptio: Supply chain end-to-end, inventory management (JIT, EOQ), transport and distribution, and international logistics (incoterms, customs).
routes.escola.curso.logistica.title: Logistics
routes.escola.curso.marketing-internacio: The applied course on international marketing: how to segment country markets (country markets, intermarket vs intra-market), targeting (undifferentiated, concentrated, differentiated, micromarketing mix) and global positioning. How to adapt the marketing-mix across countries — product (adaptation vs standardisation, country-of-origin effects), price (dumping, transfer pricing, currency hedging, PPP), place (indirect/direct export, JV, subsidiaries) and promotion (glocal messaging). How to choose entry modes (export, licensing, franchising, joint ventures, wholly-owned subsidiaries, FDI greenfield vs acquisition, alliances, born globals), framed by the Uppsala model and transaction cost analysis. And how to navigate cultural differences in international business — Hofstede's 6 dimensions (PDI, IDV, MAS, UAI, LTO, IND), CSAs (cultural sensitives), glocal strategies, corporate diplomacy and soft power.
Business Administration · Global STP, international 4Ps mix, market entry modes, Hofstede and corporate diplomacy
routes.escola.curso.negociacao.descripti: BATNA, ZOPA, Fisher & Ury principled negotiation, communication skills and cross-cultural negotiation (Hofstede, Hall).
routes.escola.curso.negociacao.title: Business Negotiation Techniques
routes.escola.curso.pesquisa-de-marketin: Research types, qualitative vs quantitative methods, sampling, questionnaires and data analysis.
Business Administration · Research process, qualitative vs quantitative, sampling, data collection methods and analysis
routes.escola.curso.sistemas-de-informac: Information systems in organisations: types (TPS, MIS, DSS, ESS), infrastructure (hardware, software, data, people, processes and network), ERP vs CRM vs SCM vs BI, integration and data flow, business intelligence (data warehouse, OLAP, dashboards, data mining), information security (confidentiality, integrity, availability, GDPR, authentication, authorisation, cryptography), cloud computing (SaaS, PaaS, IaaS, hybrid) and digital transformation with IS as competitive advantage.
Business Administration · IS types, ERP/CRM, BI, information security and cloud
routes.escola.quiz.ccq.description: Test your knowledge in consumer behaviour: factors influencing the buying decision (cultural, social, personal and psychological), Kotler's five-stage buying decision process (need recognition, information search, evaluation, purchase, post-purchase) with limited and extensive problem solving, STP segmentation (demographic, psychographic, behavioural, geographic, by benefits) and persona construction, and digital omnichannel behaviour (customer journey, mobile-first, social commerce, showrooming, webrooming, RFM and personalisation under GDPR). 10 questions, 4 options each, with detailed explanation.
routes.escola.quiz.ccq.title: Quiz: Consumer Behaviour
routes.escola.quiz.ecq.description: Test your knowledge in advanced corporate strategy: external analysis (PESTEL, Porter Five Forces, value chain, strategic groups), internal analysis (VRIO, RBV, strategic SWOT, McKinsey 7S), corporate strategies (Ansoff, BCG/GE-McKinsey, vertical/horizontal integration, diversification) and implementation (Balanced Scorecard, OKRs, change management). 10 questions, 4 options each, with detailed explanation.
routes.escola.quiz.ecq.title: Quiz: Advanced Corporate Strategy
routes.escola.quiz.epq.description: Test your knowledge on entrepreneurial mindset, Business Model Canvas, problem validation, MVP, business plan and investor pitch. 10 questions, 4 options each, with detailed explanation.
routes.escola.quiz.epq.title: Quiz: Entrepreneurship and Business Plan
routes.escola.quiz.mkq.description: Test your knowledge in international marketing: global STP segmentation (country markets, inter/intra-market, targeting, positioning), global 4Ps marketing-mix (product with country-of-origin, price with dumping/transfer pricing/hedging, place with distribution channels, glocal promotion), entry modes (export, licensing, franchising, JV, subsidiary, FDI, born globals, Uppsala and transaction cost), and Hofstede's 6 cultural dimensions (PDI, IDV, MAS, UAI, LTO, IND) with CSAs, corporate diplomacy and soft power. 10 questions, 4 options each, with detailed explanation.
routes.escola.quiz.mkq.title: Quiz: International Marketing
routes.escola.quiz.pmq.description: Test your knowledge in marketing research: 6-step research process (problem definition, plan, data collection, analysis, findings and decision), primary vs secondary data, qualitative vs quantitative, three research types (exploratory, descriptive, causal), probability and non-probability sampling, margin of error and confidence intervals, focus groups and in-depth interviews, ethnography, netnography and projective techniques (laddering, ZMET), regression (linear and logistic), factor analysis, clusters and conjoint analysis, and final report structure with executive summary and recommendations. 10 questions, 4 options each, with detailed explanation.
routes.escola.quiz.pmq.title: Quiz: Marketing Research
routes.escola.quiz.siq.description: Test your knowledge on information systems: IS types (TPS, MIS, DSS, ESS), infrastructure (hardware, software, data, people, processes, network), ERP and CRM as integration platforms, SCM and data flow, business intelligence (data warehouse, ETL, OLAP, dashboards, data mining), information security (CIA triad, GDPR, authentication, authorisation, cryptography, risk management) and cloud computing (SaaS, PaaS, IaaS, hybrid). 10 questions, 4 options each, with detailed explanation.
routes.escola.quiz.siq.title: Quiz: Management Information Systems

secret.fact.1: 🧴 The modern perfume industry was born in France in the 14th century — the word 'perfume' comes from the Latin 'per fumum' (through smoke).
secret.fact.2: 🌸 The top note is what you smell in the first 15 minutes; the heart note lasts 3–4 hours; the base note stays on the skin for up to 24 h.
secret.fact.3: 💎 Chanel N°5 (1921) was the first abstract perfume — Coco Chanel picked it because it was the 5th bottle Ernest Beaux showed her.
secret.fact.4: 🧪 Perfume marketing lives on storytelling: 80% of the price goes to packaging, brand, and distribution — the liquid is a fraction of the cost.
secret.fact.5: 🇹🇳 Tunisia has an ancestral tradition in perfumery — the city of Kairouan is known as the 'city of a thousand fountains' and its local jasmine perfume.
secret.lead: Welcome! Here are 5 facts about perfume — unlocked by your ❤️ + 🐷.

walk.audio.intro: Listen while you review. English voice, 3 tracks covering the 5 sections.
walk.audio.pers.label: Persona + Marketing Problem
walk.audio.problem.label: Persona + Marketing Problem
walk.audio.problem.title: Sections 2 + 3
walk.audio.swot.label: SWOT + Persona
walk.audio.swot.title: Sections 1 + 2
walk.audio.tows.label: TOWS + Recommendation
walk.audio.tows.title: Sections 4 + 5
walk.step.persona.how: Anchored in case study research. Add a paragraph on «Strategic Importance» explaining why this persona matters.
walk.step.persona.title: Buyer Persona
walk.step.persona.what: One primary persona with demographics, psychographics, behavior, frustrations.
walk.step.problem.how: Show your reasoning. End with a clear problem statement (1-2 sentences) that the recommendation will solve.
walk.step.problem.title: Marketing Problem
walk.step.problem.what: Formulate the most urgent problem in SCQA.
walk.step.rec.how: Why this one (and not the others). Feasibility check. Implementation plan with leadership, resources, timeline.
walk.step.rec.title: Strategic Recommendation
walk.step.rec.what: Choose one TOWS strategy and defend it.
walk.step.swot.hint: NPS 17 vs Druni 56 is a weakness that also serves as a bridge to the Marketing Problem.
walk.step.swot.how: 4-5 bullets per quadrant. Cite the case study (Elson, 2024). Be specific with numbers.
walk.step.swot.title: SWOT Analysis
walk.step.tows.how: Each cell = 1 strategy in 1 line. Then a paragraph explaining why SO wins (or whichever you choose).
walk.step.tows.title: TOWS Matrix
walk.step.tows.what: 2×2 matrix. 4 strategic alternatives. Brief evaluation of each.
walk.sub: What the professor expects in each section — and where to spend your time wisely.
walkthrough.audio.missing: No audio file for this lesson.
